title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Le Pen launches campaign after court ruling
slides.explanation: Marine Le Pen has officially started her presidential campaign just hours after a court upheld a guilty verdict against her. She remains defiant despite the legal challenges.

slides.headline: Trump declares Iran ceasefire over
slides.explanation: President Trump says the ceasefire with Iran is finished following a series of recent attacks. This escalation puts new strain on the agreement meant to stop the conflict.

slides.headline: Fatal shooting by task force in Memphis
slides.explanation: A member of a federal task force shot and killed a person at a hotel in Memphis this week. This is the second fatal shooting involving the task force in just four days.

slides.headline: Court clears path for Le Pen 2027 run
slides.explanation: A recent court decision has confirmed that Marine Le Pen is allowed to run in the 2027 French presidential election. Her campaign is now moving forward despite ongoing legal issues.

slides.headline: Le Pen faces uphill battle
slides.explanation: Marine Le Pen is pushing ahead with her 2027 presidential bid while dealing with an embezzlement conviction. Some critics are drawing parallels between her situation and the political style of Donald Trump.

caption: From major political developments in France to rising tensions with Iran, here is what you need to know today. We are breaking down the biggest headlines to keep you informed. Tap through to see the latest updates. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Meta AI uses your profile photos
slides.explanation: Meta is letting users create AI images using public Instagram profile pictures. Privacy experts are worried about this, even though there is an option to opt out.

slides.headline: AI boom hits San Francisco housing
slides.explanation: Housing prices in San Francisco have hit a record high of $1.7 million. Many experts link this jump to the influx of high-paid tech workers in the AI industry.

slides.headline: Meta's new 'always-on' glasses
slides.explanation: Reports suggest Meta is developing smart glasses that record audio and photos continuously. The goal is to let users ask Meta AI questions about their day based on what the glasses captured.

slides.headline: Boosting chip production in Europe
slides.explanation: A German startup called QuantumDiamonds is using new tech to inspect chips faster. They are being supported by the European Chips Act, which uses state funding to help local semiconductor companies.

slides.headline: Apple loses major EU court case
slides.explanation: Apple failed to challenge new European rules that label it a gatekeeper. This means the company will be forced to open its App Store up to more third-party competition.

caption: From rising housing costs in San Francisco to new privacy debates at Meta, here is what is moving the needle in tech today. Stay in the loop with the most important updates in your feed. Download Headlinne to get the full story. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
